quizzes.lessonTitle: EMC targets by NCC climate zone
quizzes.title: Psychrometry & Building Science for Drying — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Which four measurements together describe the state of moist air for drying decisions?
options: Colour, odour, texture and weight
Temperature, relative humidity, dew point, and specific humidity (g/kg)
Wind speed, air pressure, rainfall and cloud cover
Only relative humidity, read at the start and end of the job

questions.questionText: Why can relative humidity (RH) alone be misleading when judging how much water is in the air?
options: RH cannot be measured with a standard hygrometer
RH depends on temperature, so the same mass of water in the air shows a different RH reading as temperature rises or falls
RH is only relevant to outdoor air, never indoor air
RH and dew point always move in opposite directions
The temperature at which air becomes saturated (100% RH) and begins to condense moisture
The maximum safe operating temperature for a dehumidifier
The average daily temperature recorded by the Bureau of Meteorology
A fixed value that never changes regardless of how much water is in the air

questions.questionText: In Australian practice, specific humidity (the actual mass of water carried by the air) is expressed in:
options: Relative humidity as a percentage (%)
Grams of water per kilogram of dry air (g/kg)
Litres per square metre
Degrees Celsius (°C)

questions.questionText: What does a psychrometric chart or digital psychrometric calculator let a technician do?
options: Replace the need for a hygrometer entirely
Enter any two known air properties (such as temperature and RH) and read off the related properties, such as dew point and specific humidity
Predict the exact day the job will finish
Measure moisture content inside a solid material without a moisture meter

questions.questionText: What causes water vapour to move out of a wet material and into the surrounding air?
options: A vapour pressure gradient — vapour pressure at the wet surface is higher than in the surrounding air, so moisture moves from high to low pressure
Gravity pulling the water molecules downward
The material’s colour absorbing more heat
Air pressure remaining exactly equal everywhere in the building

questions.questionText: What does equilibrium moisture content (EMC) describe?
options: The moisture content a material settles at once it is in balance with the surrounding temperature and RH
The moisture content of a material the moment it gets wet
A single fixed percentage that applies to every material in every building
The reading taken only on the last day of a drying job

questions.questionText: How should a technician use EMC when setting a dry standard on a real job?
options: Apply a fixed moisture percentage that applies to every building, regardless of material or climate
Measure the same material type in an unaffected, comparable area of the building and use that reading as the drying target
Estimate the dry standard from memory without taking any reference reading
Use the manufacturer’s rated output figure for the dehumidifier as the drying target

questions.questionText: Which three readings should be logged at the same time each day to prove a drying job is progressing?
options: Only the drying space reading, taken once at the start of the job
The drying space, an unaffected control area, and outside the building
The dehumidifier’s power draw, the air mover speed setting, and the room floor area
The insurer’s claim number, the job address, and the technician’s name

questions.questionText: How do Bureau of Meteorology (BoM) climatology and NCC climate zones inform an Australian drying plan?
options: They replace the need to take any readings on site
They provide regional humidity/dew-point context and typical EMC expectations, which support and explain the on-site dry standard reading — not replace it
They only apply to new-building design, never to restoration drying
They set a single national EMC target that applies regardless of location
